claim_text: Sections 103 and 105 of the provided context discuss the punishment for murder.
Section 103 states that whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
When a group of five or more persons acting in concert commits murder on certain grounds, each member of such a group is subject to the same severe punishments as an individual murderer.

Section 105 explains that whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder can be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than five years but which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
Murder without direct intent to kill is still considered a serious offense but is punished less severely than actual murder.
